content.problemStatement:
You are given a simple undirected graph with NNN vertices and MMM edges.

Find the smallest non-negative integer KKK such that for every vertex uuu (1‚Č§u‚Č§N1 \leq u \leq N1‚Č§u‚Č§N), there exists a walk of length **exactly** KKK from 111 to uuu.

If no such integer exists, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 instead.

**Notes:**


It is allowed to repeat both vertices and edges in a walk.
The length of a walk equals the number of edges in it.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
The next MMM lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the minimum value of KKK such that there exists a length-KKK walk from 111 to every other vertex; or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if no such KKK exists.
constraints: ui‚Č†viu_i \neq v_iui‚ÄčÓÄ†=vi‚Äč for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \leq i \leq M1‚Č§i‚Č§M.
Each unordered pair (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) appears at most once.
The sum of NNN across all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105.
The sum of MMM across all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We have the following length-222 walks reaching every vertex:

1‚Üí2‚Üí31\to 2\to 31‚Üí2‚Üí3 to reach 333.
1‚Üí3‚Üí21\to 3\to 21‚Üí3‚Üí2 to reach 222.
1‚Üí2‚Üí11\to 2\to 11‚Üí2‚Üí1 to reach 111.
Note that we reuse an edge here, which is allowed.

Test case 222: It can be proved that no integer KKK satisfying the condition exists.
